'''Labansat''' was a well-known [[Weequay/Legends|Weequay]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] active during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Fierce, brutal, and a skilled fighter, Labansat operated out of the [[Lesser Plooriod Cluster/Legends|Lesser Plooriod Cluster]] and the [[Karstaxon Regions]], plying the two areas of [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. He found employment in the services of whomever agreed to his asking price, including the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].

==Biography==
An infamous [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]],<ref name="GG12" /> Labansat was a [[Weequay/Legends|Weequay]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] active during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|war]] between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. He frequently harassed two areas of [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Realspace/Legends|space]], the [[Karstaxon Regions]] and the [[Lesser Plooriod Cluster/Legends|Lesser Plooriod Cluster]], where he often found work, provided his price be met by his potential employer.<ref name="Rebellion" /> Labansat was one of many mercenaries on the payroll of the Empire.<ref name="Guide">[[Star Wars: Rebellion: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Rebellion'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref>

Labansat was briefly mentioned as a notable Weequay in the first volume of ''[[The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life]]'', a document on a variety of [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Species/Legends|species]] compiled by [[Iyra]] [[sentientologist]] [[Tem Eliss]] of the [[University of Sanbra]].<ref name="GG12" />

==Personality and traits==
Labansat was a notorious mercenary,<ref name="GG12" /> known for his viciousness and plying of the Lesser Plooriod [[Star cluster/Legends|Cluster]] and the Karstaxon Regions. A skilled and [[Death/Legends|deadly]] combatant, he required that any potential employer meet his asking price before he accepted a job opportunity.<ref name="Rebellion" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Weequay mercenary Labansat was first mentioned as a "notable personalit[y]" of his species in the [[1995]] [[West End Games]] sourcebook ''[[Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens — Enemies and Allies]]''.<ref name="GG12" /> He later appeared in the [[1998]] [[Star Wars video games|PC game]] [[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']] as one of several Imperial-aligned characters available to the player.<ref name="Rebellion" /> In the game, Labansat possesses advanced combat and [[Spy/Legends|espionage]] abilities, performing well on abduction, [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]], and [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotage]] missions; however, as a result, his leadership and [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomacy]] skills are lacking.<ref name="Guide" /> His name was later mentioned as an example of a Weequay name in ''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]'', published in [[2003]].<ref name="UAA">''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]''</ref>

[[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 10|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 10]] from [[January]] [[1997]] included an article written by [[Tom Byron]] previewing ''Star Wars: Rebellion'' prior to the game's release. The article mistakenly states that Labansat originated in author [[Timothy Zahn]]'s [[Star Wars: The Thrawn Trilogy|''Thrawn Trilogy'']] novel series.<ref name="SWGM10">{{GalaxyCite|10|Game Room|Game Room: Launching a New Rebellion}}</ref>
